article_title: Google Quantum AI Demonstrates 20-Fold Reduction in Qubits Needed to Break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, Accelerating Post-Quantum Migration Timeline

content_preview.title: Google Quantum AI Demonstrates 20-Fold Reduction in Qubits Needed to Break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, Accelerating Post-Quantum Migration Timeline
content_preview.summary: A Google Quantum AI whitepaper shows fewer than 500,000 qubits could break elliptic curve cryptography in minutes, a 20-fold improvement over prior estimates that accelerates the post-quantum migration timeline.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## The Paper

Google Quantum AI published a [whitepaper on March 30](https://research.google/blog/safeguarding-cryptocurrency-by-disclosing-quantum-vulnerabilities-responsibly/) presenting optimized quantum circuits for solving the 256-bit elliptic curve discrete logarithm problem (ECDLP-256), the mathematical foundation of the digital signatures used by Bitcoin, Ethereum, and most modern cryptographic systems. The research, authored by Ryan Babbush, Director of Research for Quantum Algorithms, ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Outstanding quantum computing/cryptography article with detailed technical specs, historical context, and policy implications.
editor_notes.source_verification: Google Research Blog: verified — Babbush/Neven, 500K qubits, 1200 logical qubits, 90M Toffoli gates, zero-knowledge proof, 2029 deadline, Ethereum Foundation confirmed. The Quantum Insider: verified — three papers context, Gidney RSA, Iceberg Quantum, 41% Bitcoin probability, Year of Quantum Security, BIP-360 confirmed. arXiv: not separately fetched but DOI format valid.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All technical claims verified across both primary sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technically precise. Appropriate urgency without alarmism.
editor_notes.originality: Fresh topic — no prior quantum cryptography coverage.
